response.justification: James brings genuine depth in high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic) and direct, current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, which maps well to the role's AI-native engineering mindset. However, Python is entirely absent from the resume — both the skills section and all role descriptions — which is a listed must-have, not a nice-to-have. TypeScript coverage is strong. Staff-level titling is present but the current Staff role is very recent (April 2025), making it difficult to assess depth at that level. The polyglot breadth is real but also raises questions about depth in the specific stack the role demands.
strengths: Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience: micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic using async I/O, caching, and low-latency design

Direct, hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work in the current role at re:cinq, including agentic workflow frameworks — aligns with the role's baseline expectation of comfort in AI-native environments

Long track record of software craftsmanship (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, pairing) and community knowledge-sharing, suggesting an ownership and engineering-standards mindset consistent with Staff-level expectations
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume — not mentioned in any role, project, or skills section — yet it is listed as a must-have technical skill for the position

The current Staff Engineer role at re:cinq began in April 2025, meaning there is only approximately one month of tenure at that level, making it hard to evaluate whether the technical-strategy and cross-functional coordination responsibilities have been demonstrated at scale

No explicit mention of operational ownership, on-call rotation, incident response, or monitoring practices — all of which are specifically called out as must-have non-technical requirements in the job description
